text_fields.bf3bdadc-f9d6-4130-bd9d-09c7244a6d4c_1a1: No hard deadlines on my end. I would need roughly two weeks from an offer to wrap things up at Microsoft, so my earliest start would be two weeks from whenever we close.
text_fields.bf3bdadc-f9d6-4130-bd9d-09c7244a6d4c_1ca:
The work I have been doing at Microsoft maps directly onto what this role is asking for. I built an internal Resilience Automation Platform from scratch, defined the deployment lifecycle and self-service scheduling workflows, and drove down the toil it took to run 45+ annual drills. I also built an AI agent that restructured end-to-end planning workflows, cutting cycle time by 39%. Those are exactly the muscles this role exercises: collapsing time-to-production, making complex infrastructure self-serve, and treating the agent as a first-class user rather than an afterthought. I have been on the customer side of deploy-and-operate friction, and I know how much a coherent CLI, a clean deployment lifecycle, and visible build progress actually matter.

Baseten is doing something specific that I find genuinely compelling: you are making inference infrastructure a solved problem for the companies actually pushing the frontier, and you are doing it by betting on developer experience as a first-class product surface, not an afterthought. The fact that Cursor, Notion, and Abridge run on Baseten tells me the bar for reliability and DevEx is already high, and the Series F signals you have the runway to go much further. Being one of the first PMs to define what great product looks like at a company with this technical depth and this customer base is exactly the kind of 0-to-1 opportunity I am looking for.
text_fields.bf3bdadc-f9d6-4130-bd9d-09c7244a6d4c_443:
The work I am most proud of is building Microsoft's first internal Resilience Automation Platform from zero. Before it existed, running a resilience drill meant a two-person operation handling scheduling, coordination, and execution almost entirely by hand. I owned the product end-to-end: defined the self-service scheduling requirements, mapped the deployment lifecycle, and pushed through the cross-org alignment needed to get 140+ engineering teams operating against a shared model. The result was a 30% reduction in operational toil and a platform that scaled that two-person operation to 45+ annual drills.

What makes this most relevant to the Baseten role is not just the outcome but how I got there. I ran structured discovery with the teams doing the actual work, surfaced where the friction lived in the deploy-and-operate loop, and turned that into roadmap. I also built an AI agent for drill planning that cut planning cycle time by 39% and increased execution capacity by 21%, which required treating the agent as a real user of the tooling rather than a bolt-on feature. That is the exact instinct this role calls for: collapsing time-to-production, converting work that needs specialist support into something a developer does alone, and making the agent a first-class citizen in how the product is designed.

The other piece I would call out is the parallel work I did during my 2022 internship, where I ran discovery with 20+ service teams, identified 81 hours of manual toil per region launch, and built a roadmap to automate those critical paths. That pattern, finding the truth in customer conversations and converting friction into self-serve developer workflows, is what I want to keep doing, just at the infrastructure layer where the stakes are highest.
text_fields.bf3bdadc-f9d6-4130-bd9d-09c7244a6d4c_de6:
I want to flag something directly: I don't have access to Cyrus Shekari's actual resume, work history, or biographical details. If I were to write a specific, grounded answer here, I would be inventing employers, titles, and accomplishments, which is exactly what I should not do.

What I can do is help you write this answer well if you share the real details. If you give me Cyrus's actual background, including relevant roles, what he has built or shipped, his experience with developer tools or ML infrastructure, and anything specific about why Baseten appeals to him, I can turn that into a sharp, confident "additional information" response that sounds like him and makes a strong case for the role.

What would you like to share about his background?

radios.name: bf3bdadc-f9d6-4130-bd9d-09c7244a6d4c_0806bd61-76d1-48b7-8985-24c6fa5f0d46
radios.label: Our offices are located in San Francisco, CA and New York City. Please indicate which location you are interested in?

radios.name: bf3bdadc-f9d6-4130-bd9d-09c7244a6d4c_bdc286d0-ecbc-4825-bd2a-7abba8d67f06
radios.label: Will you  require employment visa sponsorship to work in the country in which the job you're applying for is located (either now or in the future)?

radios.name: bf3bdadc-f9d6-4130-bd9d-09c7244a6d4c_f38eb9c7-eeaf-4509-84f6-278558a315aa
radios.label: Have you ever interviewed at Baseten before?

cover_overrides.label: do you have any deadlines or timeline considerations we should be aware of? what is the earliest you would want to start working with us?
why do you want to work at baseten?
walk us through your proudest achievement and how it relates to the role you are applying for.

args.label: baseten-2d78fdcf-53e1-45d3-a047-2aefb5ad3153
args.comment: Resolve dryrun field ids -> actual DOM ids (Ashby sometimes prefixes with form id).

args.label: baseten-2d78fdcf-53e1-45d3-a047-2aefb5ad3153
args.comment:
chain_005 P1 (2026-05-26): native-setter FAST PATH. Tries
`_valueTracker.setValue` + input-event for each plain text
field. Chain_004 verified this DOES propagate React state
for Ashby String/Email/Phone/Number/Url/LongText on Sentry
& OpenAI tenants (5–10x faster than CDP keystrokes).
Driver MUST verify by re-reading each input.value after a
~50ms tick; any field whose read-back != requested value
falls through to the CDP-keystroke step below. Combobox /
Location fields are intentionally NOT in text_fields
(they go through ashby.location_typeahead_fill instead).
Kill switch: set ashby_filler.USE_NATIVE_SETTER_FAST_PATH=False.

args.label: baseten-2d78fdcf-53e1-45d3-a047-2aefb5ad3153

args.comment:
FIX 1 (Harvey 671): for each (resolved_dom_id, value), driver must
  1. snapshot the input (ref by `#<dom_id>`), 2. act:click on the
  ref to focus, 3. clear via Ctrl+A then Delete keys, 4. act:type the
  value as REAL CDP keystrokes (NOT via JS native value setter).
chain_005 P1 (2026-05-26): driver should SKIP fields that the
fast-path step above already populated successfully. Only fields
whose read-back value != requested need CDP keystrokes.
date_fields (see below) ALSO go through this step — driver should
send an extra Enter key after typing each date_field value to
commit the react-datepicker selection.

args.label: baseten-2d78fdcf-53e1-45d3-a047-2aefb5ad3153
args.comment:
FIX 2 (Harvey 671): resolve each radio group -> matching <label>
element id + bounding-rect center. Returns {picked: [{label_id, cx, cy, label_text, kind}]}.
chain_033 (2026-05-30 Notion fix): `kind` is either `radio_label`
(traditional <input type=radio>) or `yesno_button` (Notion / Speak /
Baseten / Plain style — <div class=_yesno_>Yes/No buttons + hidden
checkbox). Driver behavior is IDENTICAL for both kinds: scrollIntoView
on the element + `act:clickCoords` at (cx, cy). Do NOT use JS .click()
on the <input> — Ashby React validator ignores synthetic clicks.
Post-click verify (chain_033): re-evaluate JS_VERIFY —
`yesno_buttons_active` should equal `yesno_groups` (each Boolean field
now has an active button). If not, retry the missing entry's clickCoords.

args.label: baseten-2d78fdcf-53e1-45d3-a047-2aefb5ad3153
args.comment:
FIX 3 (Harvey 671): driver must NOT use selector='#_systemfield_resume'
(silently no-ops on multi-file-input pages). Instead:
  1. browser.snapshot to get the page.
  2. Find the 'Upload File' button whose nearest ancestor label/
     section text contains 'Resume' (and NOT 'Transcript' or
     'Cover Letter' or other file-field labels).
  3. browser.upload ref=<that_button_ref> paths=[...].
  4. Verify by re-snapshot: page should show the resume filename.
FALLBACK (single file input on page): browser.upload
  element='#_systemfield_resume' paths=[...]. MUST use `element=`,
  NOT `selector=` — selector= silently no-ops with files=0.
  See _upload-regression-diag-20260525.md (2026-05-25).
POST-UPLOAD VERIFY (required): evaluate
  () => document.querySelector('#_systemfield_resume')?.files?.length || 0
  If 0, treat as UploadFailedError and retry once with element= form.

args.label: baseten-2d78fdcf-53e1-45d3-a047-2aefb5ad3153
args.comment: Verify form state: counts of filled inputs, picked radios/checkboxes, attached files, submit button presence.

args.label: baseten-2d78fdcf-53e1-45d3-a047-2aefb5ad3153
args.comment:
FIX 5 (strict-Ashby reCAPTCHA v3): driver should
  1. evaluate `() => Array.from(document.scripts).map(s=>s.src).filter(s=>s.includes('recaptcha'))`
     to confirm the v3 loader + extract sitekey from `?render=...`.
  2. If sitekey == known_strict_sitekey (Ashby shared infra), call
     captcha_solver.CaptchaSolver(vendor='capsolver').solve_recaptcha_v3(
       sitekey, page_url, page_action='submit', min_score=0.7)
  3. On token: evaluate to inject —
     `(t) => { const ids=['g-recaptcha-response','g-recaptcha-response-100000'];
      for (const id of ids) { let el=document.getElementById(id); if (!el){
        el=document.createElement('textarea'); el.id=id; el.name=id; el.style.display='none';
        document.body.appendChild(el);} el.value=t;} return {injected: ids.length};}`
  4. THEN click Submit. If SolverNotConfigured — skip (log
     `captcha-skipped-no-key`); submit will likely spam-flag but
     attempt is still required per per-role doctrine.
  5. If post-submit DOM still shows `[role=alert]` containing
     `flagged as possible spam`, the v3 score was sub-threshold;
     log per-tenant + bail (no infinite retry loop).
NEW 2026-05-27: easiest path is to just call
  `from captcha_presubmit import solve_and_inject_recaptcha_v3;
   solve_and_inject_recaptcha_v3(frame, **driver_exec.kwargs)`
  which handles detect+solve+inject in one call. No-op when
  ENABLE_CAPSOLVER!=1 — safe to leave wired permanently.

args.label: baseten-2d78fdcf-53e1-45d3-a047-2aefb5ad3153
args.comment: FINAL: click 'Submit Application'. Run only after VERIFY passes manual review.
